body_txt: Whether you're there for a trim, line-up, a shampoo-and-set, a perm, a dye job, or possibly a facial or manicure, the Chatty Hairdresser (or Barber) will be there to help you out and talk your ear off. Compliments, quirky personal anecdotes, urban legends, the latest gossip , life lessons, and romantic advice come free with the purchase of any salon service, whether you want it or not. If you're lucky, it's just comic relief. If not, plot may come crashing down on your head. This trope is particularly prevalent in stories set in the Black community. Due to the unique needs and styles of afro-textured hair, Black people often prefer hairstylists who specialize in its care. This has led to Black salons and barbershops becoming sacred, neutral spaces in the community. Rich or poor, gangster or preacher, everyone needs to get their hair done at some point. As such, these spaces are major rumor mills. Compare Driver of a Black Cab , who is a chatty cabdriver. She (or he) may also be found in or the cause of a Sucky Salon Visit . noreallife
